evidence_summary: Batch normalization can be applied to any set of activations in the network, including recurrent architectures, but its effect is dependent on the mini-batch size.
Layer normalization is a technique that can be used to normalize the activities of neurons in recurrent neural networks, and it has been shown to reduce internal covariate shift.
Gradient clipping is a method that can be used to stabilize learning in recurrent neural networks by preventing exploding gradients.
